subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Identify acids and bases using natural and synthetic indicators
Understand the basic chemical properties of acids and bases
Recognize the importance of pH in everyday life
Understand the neutralization reaction and salt formation
Identify common chemicals prepared from Sodium Chloride
Explain the importance of Water of Crystallization

lesson_plan.title: Indicators of Acids and Bases
topics_covered: Definition of Acids and Bases (Taste and Touch)
Natural indicators: Litmus, Turmeric, Red Cabbage
Synthetic indicators: Methyl Orange, Phenolphthalein
teaching_strategy: Hands-on demonstration with Lemon juice, Soap, and Litmus paper
Magic color-change experiment using Turmeric
Visual chart of indicator color changes
student_activities: Test various household liquids (vinegar, soda, soap) with Litmus
Create a table of color changes observed
assessment: Matching activity: Substance to Acid/Base category

lesson_plan.title: Chemical Properties: Metals and Carbonates
topics_covered: Reaction of Acids with Metals ($H_{2}$ gas test)
Reaction of Acids with Metal Carbonates ($CO_{2}$ gas test)
The 'Pop' test and Lime Water test
teaching_strategy: Lab demonstration: Zinc granules in dilute $H_{2}SO_{4}$
Passing $CO_{2}$ through Lime water to show milkiness
Simple word equations
student_activities: Draw the experimental setup for gas evolution
Record the observation for the 'Pop' sound
assessment: Short answer: Which gas is evolved when acid reacts with metal?

lesson_plan.title: Neutralization and pH Scale
topics_covered: The reaction between Acid and Base
Concept of Salt and Water formation
The pH scale (0-14) and Universal Indicator
teaching_strategy: Analogy: Balancing a see-saw
Demonstration of mixing $HCl$ and $NaOH$ with Phenolphthalein
Color-coded pH strip chart
student_activities: Use pH paper to test the acidity of coffee, milk, and water
Color the pH scale in their notebooks
assessment: True/False on pH values (e.g., pH 2 is a strong acid)
Exit slip: What are the products of neutralization?

lesson_plan.title: pH in Everyday Life
topics_covered: pH in our digestive system (Acidity and Antacids)
Tooth decay and pH change
pH sensitivity of plants and animals (Soil pH)
teaching_strategy: Storytelling: Why do we take 'Milk of Magnesia'?
Discussion on how toothpaste prevents cavities
Visual aids showing yellowing plants in acidic soil
student_activities: Group talk: Home remedies for bee stings
List 3 ways pH affects our daily routine
assessment: Oral round: Why do we brush our teeth?

lesson_plan.title: Salts from Sodium Chloride
topics_covered: Common Salt ($NaCl$) as a raw material
Sodium Hydroxide (Chlor-alkali process - basic)
Bleaching Powder and Baking Soda
teaching_strategy: Displaying samples of salt and baking soda
Simplified flowchart of the Chlor-alkali process
Uses of Baking Soda in the kitchen (fluffy cakes)
student_activities: Write the common names of $NaHCO_{3}$ and $CaOCl_{2}$
List two uses for Bleaching Powder
assessment: Fill in the blanks: Chemical names and formulas

lesson_plan.title: Washing Soda and Plaster of Paris
topics_covered: Washing Soda ($Na_{2}CO_{3} \cdot 10H_{2}O$)
Plaster of Paris ($CaSO_{4} \cdot \frac{1}{2}H_{2}O$)
Water of Crystallization
teaching_strategy: Showing a plaster cast or gypsum sample
Heating Copper Sulphate crystals to show color loss
Explaining why POP must be stored in moisture-proof containers
student_activities: Observe the change in blue Copper Sulphate upon heating
Identify uses of POP in construction and medicine

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Assessment
topics_covered: Summary of Indicators, pH, and Salts
Formula recap
Doubt clarification
teaching_strategy: Quiz-based game
Mind-map construction on the board
Rapid-fire formula round
student_activities: Complete a summary table for all salts covered
assessment: MCQs on indicators and salt formulas

teaching_aids: Samples: Lemon, Vinegar, Baking Soda, Salt, POP
teacher_tip: Keep the focus on household examples. Using kitchen items like vinegar and baking soda makes the 'scary' chemistry feel familiar and accessible for Class 10 students starting the chapter.
